data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS, no findings.

T11 fires only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data. This epic's "migrate" usage is code/orchestration reorganization (WS-MIGRATE: moving verify_completion + the review reviewers onto the workflow-engine substrate), not a persisted-data migration — consistent with rebar's house meaning of "migration" (the bash→Python strangler), which the gate's own experiment harnesses explicitly note makes the deterministic T11 keyword false-fire (see docs/experiments/.../gate_lib.py, analyze_e4.py, make_crit_v8.py). WS-PROMPTS further states the experiment criteria_v*.json are explicitly NOT migrated/moved ("no migration of prototypes"). The only contemplated persisted-FORMAT change is WS-VISUAL (adopt a workflow format/technology with a visual-editing ecosystem), but (a) it is explicitly undecided and deferred to the brainstorm, and (b) the affected artifacts are small, git-tracked, schema-validated config-as-code (the workflow YAML DSL under src/rebar/llm/workflow/), not a large persisted data store — none of T11's execution-safety checks (online expand-contract, batched/throttled backfill, dual-write window, downtime DDL) apply to a version-controlled text-format conversion with full git rollback. Affirmation: the plan's overlay-routing is sound — it correctly distinguishes a code-substrate "migration" from a data-migration and explicitly disclaims moving the experiment prototypes.
